reasoning: The FRED tool gives a combined probability of 0.29 for the 30-year TIPS real yield to increase beyond its highly elevated current value of 2.66%. Daily yield changes generally follow a random walk (baseline 50%), but mean reversion becomes a more significant factor at historical extremes. The February 2026 30-year TIPS auction yielded 2.47%, which was the second highest in 16 years, meaning the current 2.66% level is exceptionally high and prime for a pullback. Recent spikes appear driven by geopolitical shocks (Iran conflict), which tend to be volatile. Blending the statistical estimate with the random walk prior while heavily weighting the mean-reverting extreme level yields a final probability of 31%.
